title: engine: build status.py (queue depth/age/recent outcomes) and generalize VERIFIERS beyond one hardcoded ticket kind
prompt:
The engine (engine/orchestrator.py, engine/store/) has a genuinely good ticket schema (queue_id, requested_by, verify_kind, claimed_by/claimed_at, audit.jsonl's ticket->worker->session_id chain) but zero aggregation — answering 'what's queued, how old is the oldest ticket, did ticket X succeed' today means ls-ing store/queue/ and store/done/ and reading raw JSON by hand. Separately, orchestrator.py's VERIFIERS dict (orchestrator.py:217) has exactly ONE entry (verify_constellation_4_apps) — every other ticket kind, including the 8 tickets this evaluation is filing right now, has no automated verification path; only the worker's own self-report in results/<ticket>.json is checked.

Two pieces:

1. Build engine/status.py: prints queue depth, oldest queued ticket's age, and the last N done tickets with title + result/verified outcome, reading store/queue/, store/done/, and audit.jsonl. No new state — pure read/aggregate over the existing ledger.

2. Generalize VERIFIERS: add at minimum one generic, declarative verifier (e.g. a ticket can carry a `verify_spec: {files_changed: [...], must_contain: [...]}` block, checked by a single generic function) so a new ticket kind doesn't require hand-writing a bespoke Python function in orchestrator.py every time. Keep the existing verify_constellation_4_apps working unchanged; this is additive.
verify: python3 engine/status.py prints a correct queue depth and oldest-ticket age matching a manual ls/cat of store/queue/; VERIFIERS has more than one working entry, demonstrated against at least one of this evaluation's own 8 tickets once it reaches done/.
verified: python3 engine/status.py run against the real store: queue depth=8, oldest=20260704T034440Z-research-heterogeneous-worker-pools.json age=2.9h (10518s), cross-checked against its requested_at 2026-07-04T03:44:40Z vs generated_at 2026-07-04T06:39:58Z — matches exactly. running/ shows the actual 3 real tickets (untouched throughout). done/ shows the 2 real done tickets with outcome text pulled from results/<ticket>.json or the ticket's own result/verified fields (schema drift handled). tmux df-worker-1 and audit.jsonl tail both surfaced correctly. VERIFIERS now has 3 entries (constellation-4-apps unchanged, generic declarative checks[] DSL, artifacts fallback) backed by a CHECK_REGISTRY (file_exists/json_parses/must_contain/git_commit_present/script/command). Demonstrated via a full dry lifecycle on a throwaway dummy ticket: dropped into store/queue/, lease() (rename+ownership, same CAS mutex), simulated a worker's results/<tid>.json self-report, verify() with verify_kind=generic passed against real read-only checks (file_exists on engine/status.py, json_parses on mochaccino/data/constellation.json), commit() moved it to done/, status.py picked it up correctly — then cleaned up (removed from done/+results/) so the real ledger wasn't polluted with a fake entry. The 3 real running tickets and 8 real queue tickets were never touched. No warm pool booted, no tmux sessions created.
notes: Per ticket-first rule: results appended directly into this ticket JSON, file left in running/ (not moved to done/ by me — that's the orchestrator's reap job, gated on artifact_ok()+verify(), not a manual close). See engine/README.md's new 'Verifying a new ticket kind' section and engine/docs/ENGINE-NOTES.md's updated 'Known limitations' (both prior gaps now marked Closed 2026-07-04).
